criteria.name: build_and_run_end_to_end
criteria.description: Creating a script, adding a Navigate, a Type, an Extract, and an Assert Text step with valid parameters, saving a version, and clicking Run Script highlights the steps in order, streams one console line per step, fills the extracted-values table, updates the rollup to n of m, writes one new record into the Runs view, and shows all four steps in the Definition JSON while the Run report shows the new run results — all without a reload
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The created script did not appear in the library or editor; the visible editor remained the seeded six-step script and the console stayed at 0 events. Evidence: build_and_run_end_to_end-shared.png

criteria.name: invalid_forms_block_inline
criteria.description: Submitting New Script, a step parameter form, or the schedule form with missing or invalid required fields triggers immediate inline validation naming the field and changes no collection
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: New Script opened with required fields empty and Create script disabled, but submitting to observe immediate inline validation was not possible. Evidence: invalid_forms_block_inline-shared.png

criteria.name: failure_recovery_flow
criteria.description: When a step exhausts its retries the rollup failure count increases and the console shows the error reason; the step Retry control resumes from that step, pausing and resuming freezes and continues at the same step, and the event timeline records the retry, pause, and resume transitions in order
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No failure/retry/pause/resume path could be reached and verified. Evidence: build_and_run_end_to_end-shared.png

criteria.name: selector_to_step_flow
criteria.description: Pasting HTML into the Playground, typing a selector that highlights two matched elements with a match count of 2, and using Send to step writes that selector into the chosen step field, visible when the editor is reopened
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Playground showed 2 matches, but Send to step was disabled and no selector was written into a step. Evidence: selector_to_step_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: diff_two_runs_flow
criteria.description: Running the same script twice produces two run records whose extracted values differ; selecting both and activating Compare highlights the changed values, and comparing a run with itself shows the no-differences state
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Runs view showed three seeded runs, but no two-run selection or changed-value comparison was completed. Evidence: diff_two_runs_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: schedule_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Enabling a schedule with a time and interval shows the clock badge in the sidebar and a countdown in the Scheduled queue; Trigger now produces a schedule-triggered run record in history and the Run report tab then reflects that run totals
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The schedule dialog opened, but its Schedule switch could not be clicked because the label intercepted the pointer; no schedule/countdown/trigger flow was completed. Evidence: schedule_round_trip_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: undo_timeline_flow
criteria.description: Reordering two steps, deleting a third, then clicking the history timeline entry from before the delete restores the deleted step and its position while keeping the reorder
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: Reorder, delete, and timeline restoration could not be exercised. Evidence: build_and_run_end_to_end-shared.png

criteria.name: sidebar_collapse_continuity
criteria.description: At a narrow width the script library collapses behind a toggle and reopens as an overlay with the same entries, badges, and selection state, preserving workflow continuity
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: At narrow width the library collapsed and the Open script library control was visible, but it was blocked by the still-open schedule modal, so continuity could not be completed. Evidence: sidebar_collapse_continuity-shared.png

criteria.name: overlays_dismiss_cleanly
criteria.description: The New Script modal, screenshot modal, inline confirmations, and the palette all close via their cancel control and Escape, returning to the prior view state with no lost work outside the dismissed overlay
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Escape did not dismiss the New Script modal, and the schedule overlay also remained blocking later controls. Evidence: overlays_dismiss_cleanly-shared.png

criteria.name: flows_recover_without_reload
criteria.description: Every dead end is recoverable in-app: after deleting the selected script, emptying a script of steps, or closing the diff view, the user can continue working without a page reload
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The specified delete/empty/diff-close recovery paths were not all reachable. Evidence: build_and_run_end_to_end-shared.png

criteria.name: primary_flows_unreachable
criteria.description: Creating, editing, running, diffing, scheduling, or exporting cannot be completed from the UI alone
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: At least the schedule flow could not be completed from the UI because the visible Schedule switch was not clickable; the selector flow also stopped at a disabled Send to step control. Evidence: schedule_round_trip_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: records_lost_or_duplicated
criteria.description: A completed run fails to appear in the Runs view or appears twice
criteria.reasoning: No completed run created during grading was visibly confirmed as lost or duplicated; the Runs view showed three distinct seeded records. Evidence: diff_two_runs_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: flow_state_desyncs_views
criteria.description: Completing a flow leaves two surfaces disagreeing — for example the library step count contradicts the editor list
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Invoking browse_open for Runs reported success, but the visible page remained Export until the Runs tab was clicked manually, demonstrating a state/view mismatch. Evidence: diff_two_runs_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: view_switch_loses_selection
criteria.description: Switching between editor, Playground, Runs, Scheduled queue, and Export loses the selected script or breaks layout
criteria.reasoning: The visible UI retained Checkout confidence while switching between Editor, Playground, Runs, and Export; no selection loss was directly observed. Evidence: selector_to_step_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: emptied_script_breaks_ui
criteria.description: Removing every step produces a broken or blank editor instead of the empty state naming Add Step
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No script was emptied because the required editing flow was not completed. Evidence: build_and_run_end_to_end-shared.png

criteria.name: filtered_surfaces_disagree
criteria.description: The timeline status filter or the Runs list disagrees with the underlying run data visible elsewhere
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: No timeline or Runs filter comparison was completed. Evidence: diff_two_runs_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: collapsed_sidebar_stuck
criteria.description: The collapsed script library cannot be reopened
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The collapsed sidebar could not be reopened because an unrelated schedule modal intercepted the control; the intrinsic reopen behavior was not isolated. Evidence: sidebar_collapse_continuity-shared.png

criteria.name: overlay_traps_user
criteria.description: A modal, confirmation, or the palette blocks interaction with no keyboard or click path to dismiss it
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The schedule modal visibly intercepted the mobile library toggle and the Schedule switch, and Escape did not close the earlier New Script modal. Evidence: sidebar_collapse_continuity-shared.png

criteria.name: failed_action_silent
criteria.description: A failed or rejected action (invalid form, undo with empty stack, run on a script with no enabled steps) gives no visible feedback
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Escape on the New Script modal produced no visible dismissal or feedback, and the schedule switch click was rejected without an in-app error message. Evidence: overlays_dismiss_cleanly-shared.png

criteria.name: dead_end_requires_reload
criteria.description: Some navigation state can only be corrected by reloading the page
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: After the schedule modal became blocking, normal controls such as the mobile library toggle could not be reached and no successful in-app recovery was observed. Evidence: sidebar_collapse_continuity-shared.png

criteria.name: user_flows_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in critical end-to-end user flows that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant defect beyond the covered reachability, overlay, and view-state failures was established. Evidence: schedule_round_trip_flow-shared.png

criteria.name: layout_matches_anatomy
criteria.description: The layout shows a left sidebar with the script library and bulk bar, a main area swapping between step editor with console below, Playground, Runs, Scheduled queue, and Export views via a toolbar switcher, and a Versions panel alongside the editor
criteria.reasoning: Observed the left script library, editor with Versions panel and console, plus toolbar views for Playground, Runs, Scheduled queue, and Export. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: active_step_border_and_pulse
criteria.description: The active running step carries a 2 pixel solid brand-blue left border and a pulsing background while it runs
criteria.reasoning: During a live restart, the running step had a 2px brand-blue border and computed animation pulse-step. Evidence: active_step_border_and_pulse-early.png

criteria.name: inline_step_controls_consistent
criteria.description: Step type selectors and parameter fields render inline within each numbered step row using consistent compact controls
criteria.reasoning: Step rows visibly contain compact inline labels, selectors, and parameter inputs with consistent styling. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: console_terminal_treatment
criteria.description: The run console uses a near-black background with monospace text, pass lines in success green and fail lines in error red, and every offered theme keeps the pass/fail distinction legible
criteria.reasoning: BLOCKED: The console visibly uses a near-black background, monospace text, and green pass lines, but no fail line was rendered to verify the required red fail treatment across themes. Evidence: console_terminal_treatment-shared.png

criteria.name: clock_badge_small
criteria.description: Scheduled scripts show a small clock icon badge of roughly 12 pixels on their sidebar entry
criteria.reasoning: After enabling a schedule, the sidebar entry visibly showed a small clock badge. Evidence: clock_badge_small-scheduled.png

criteria.name: status_badges_distinct_everywhere
criteria.description: Disabled steps render dimmed with a Disabled tag, and skipped, passed, and failed badges are visually distinct and consistent between the step list, console, timeline, and Runs view
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Disabled, Pass, Running, and Retrying states were observed, but skipped and failed badges were not rendered for verification across all surfaces. Evidence: status_badges_distinct_everywhere-disabled.png

criteria.name: diff_treatments_not_color_only
criteria.description: Diff highlights use three distinct treatments for added, removed, and changed rows, each carrying an icon or label in addition to color
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Diff mode was exercised, but no added, removed, or changed row treatments with labels/icons appeared. Evidence: diff_treatments_not_color_only-version3.png

criteria.name: typography_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography shows a clear hierarchy: the app title larger than panel headings, which are larger than step rows and console text, consistent across views
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The visible app title and panel headings do not establish the required clearly decreasing hierarchy into step and console text. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: spacing_rhythm
criteria.description: Gaps between the sidebar, editor, console, and panels are visually regular with no crowded or orphaned regions
criteria.reasoning: The sidebar, editor, Versions panel, and console use regular gaps without visibly crowded or orphaned regions. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: control_state_treatments
criteria.description: Buttons, inputs, selects, and toggles show distinct default, hover, focus (visible ring), disabled, and error treatments
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Styled default, hover, and disabled states were observed, but a distinct visible focus ring and error treatment were not observed. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-export.png

criteria.name: single_icon_set
criteria.description: One consistent icon set is used throughout the toolbar, library entries, step rows, and status badges
criteria.reasoning: Toolbar, sidebar, editor controls, and badges consistently use the same compact SVG icon style. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: status_colors_incoherent
criteria.description: Status colors are inconsistent with the specified mappings — for example fail rendered green in one surface, or a console theme that erases the error color
criteria.reasoning: No incoherent status mapping was observed; visible pass states were green, running states blue, and disabled states dimmed. Evidence: status_badges_distinct_everywhere-disabled.png

criteria.name: text_overlap_or_clipping
criteria.description: At 1440px width, any heading, step row, console line, badge, or preview text overlaps another element or is clipped by its container
criteria.reasoning: At 1440px width, visible headings, rows, badges, console lines, and preview text were not overlapping or clipped. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: default_unstyled_controls
criteria.description: Form or button controls render with browser default styling where the spec styles them, or the editor reads as an unstyled wireframe rather than a designed surface
criteria.reasoning: Controls visibly use designed Carbon-style sizing, borders, colors, and spacing rather than browser defaults. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: visual_design_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in visual design that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant visual defect outside the covered criteria was observed. Evidence: layout_matches_anatomy-shared.png

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).
criteria.reasoning: Section headings use a consistent sentence-case convention, with consistently styled uppercase eyebrow labels. Evidence: copy-editor.png

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs (Run Script, Save version, Trigger now, Copy export), not generic (Submit, OK) when a specific label is possible.
criteria.reasoning: Actions use specific labels such as New Script, Save version, Run Script, Add Step, Edit schedule, and Copy export. Evidence: copy-editor.png

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages, they name the problem and the fix (Wait duration must be a positive number of milliseconds), not just a rejection (Invalid).
criteria.reasoning: No error messages were rendered during observation, so no deficient error copy was found. Evidence: copy-editor.png

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty states (no steps, no runs, no scheduled scripts, empty palette results), the copy explains what belongs there and the control that fills it.
criteria.reasoning: The empty scheduled queue explains both what belongs there and how to populate it: open a script and use Schedule. Evidence: copy-export.png

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or descriptive copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.
criteria.reasoning: Descriptive copy was clear and grammatically sound, with no observed spelling errors. Evidence: copy-playground.png

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent — the status vocabulary is exactly Pass, Fail, Skipped, Running, Retrying, and Paused everywhere, never synonyms.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Status terminology includes PASSED and FAILURES in the editor alongside Pass and Fail elsewhere, rather than using the exact required vocabulary consistently. Evidence: copy-editor.png, copy-runs.png

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, timestamps, durations, and counts, formatting is consistent (same timestamp format in console and timeline, durations with units).
criteria.reasoning: Dates use a consistent format, and durations consistently include units such as ms or s in their respective contexts. Evidence: copy-editor.png, copy-runs.png

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened (Version 4 saved, Export copied), not vague affirmations.
criteria.reasoning: Visible confirmation/status copy is specific, including Schedule enabled and Compiled from session state. Evidence: copy-success.png

criteria.name: placeholder_copy_is_visible
criteria.description: Lorem ipsum or placeholder text (TODO, Lorem, asdf, or template variable names such as {{title}}) is visible anywhere in the shipped UI.
criteria.reasoning: No Lorem ipsum, TODO, asdf, or template-variable placeholder text was visible. Evidence: copy-editor.png, copy-export-real.png

criteria.name: prominent_copy_has_errors
criteria.description: Spelling or grammatical errors appear in prominent copy such as headings, view names, or primary buttons.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The prominent editor count renders as “Steps6 total,” missing a space and producing malformed copy. Evidence: copy-editor.png

criteria.name: concept_names_are_inconsistent
criteria.description: The same concept is named inconsistently across surfaces (for example Skipped in one view and Bypassed in another).
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: The same run-status concept is rendered as Pass/Fail in run history but PASSED/FAILURES in the editor summary. Evidence: copy-editor.png, copy-runs.png

criteria.name: copy_is_truncated_or_overflowing
criteria.description: Truncated or overflowing text renders copy unreadable.
criteria.reasoning: FAIL: Multiple visible editor fields truncate copy, including “Choose featured i,” “Enter buyer email,” and “Capture order tot,” and DOM measurements show their containers narrower than their text. Evidence: copy-editor.png

criteria.name: writing_catchall
criteria.description: The app exhibits a significant, browser-observable defect in rendered copy quality that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. 'Significant' means it would plausibly matter to a real user, not a nitpick. If present, name the defect and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the defect is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.
criteria.reasoning: No additional significant copy defect was observed beyond the status terminology and truncation issues already covered above. Evidence: copy-editor.png
